title: 10 best herbal creams for jaw lock

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with jaw lock because of its calming and soothing properties that target the tension and stress in the facial muscles. The natural anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, providing immediate relief from jaw pain and stiffness. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to soften and relax the skin around the jawline, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed jaw position.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, making it easier to relax the muscles and alleviate discomfort.
Muscle-relaxing properties: The soothing and calming effects of chamomile help to relax tense jaw muscles, reducing tension and spasms that can contribute to jaw locking.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties help to calm the mind and body, which can alleviate stress-related jaw locking caused by clenching or grinding teeth due to anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm jaw muscles, reducing tension and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, an antioxidant that relaxes muscle contractions and reduces pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250g of coconut oil and 125g of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Add 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the melted mixture and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids.
Mix in 20g of vitamin e oil and 15g of aloe vera gel stirring well to combine.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized tubes filling to the top and refrigerate for 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with jaw lock because they contain soothing and calming properties that ease tension in the jaw muscles. The herbal extract of lemon balm has a natural ability to relax and calm the mind, which in turn reduces muscle contractions and spasms in the jaw. This gentle yet effective relief from anxiety and stress helps to release the grip of the jaw, alleviating the discomfort associated with jaw lock and promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream helps to relax muscle spasms in the jaw and surrounding areas, reducing tension and alleviating pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lemon balm's ability to calm the nervous system helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of jaw lock and tension headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are good for making creams as they contain high levels of volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help relieve tension in the jaw.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high amounts of rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-spasmodic effects, making them useful for reducing muscle spasms in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered melissa officinalis with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it at a low temperature for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches a warm consistency.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 5 grams of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil then stir until they are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system. The herb valerian root has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can often contribute to tense jaw muscles. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to soothe and calm the jaw area, reducing muscle spasms and tension. This results in a significant relief from jaw lock and related discomforts such as headaches and facial pain.
remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian root's sedative and muscle-relaxing compounds help to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, which can become tense and locked due to stress, anxiety, or other factors.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in valerian creams can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, a common cause of jaw lock and discomfort.
Sedative and Anxiolytic Properties: Valerian's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related tension that may contribute to jaw lock, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils that help to relax muscles and calm nervous tension, making them effective in treating jaw lock.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly of debris and impurities.
Steam distill the roots in a double boiler with 10% essential oil concentration for 3 hours to extract valerenic acid.
Combine the extracted valerenic acid with coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a water bath at 160°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the wax melts and ingredients blend.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool to room temperature before sealing and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with jaw lock because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax tense muscles. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw, temple, and surrounding areas provides instant relief for individuals experiencing TMJ disorder or bruxism. Additionally, maypop's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of jaw tension. As a result, maypop creams provide a natural solution for alleviating jaw lock, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which can contribute to jaw clenching and locking due to tension and stress relief. Its sedative effects help calm the nervous system and relax the muscles.

Lavender oil: Lavender oil has a calming and relaxing effect on the body, which can help reduce muscle tension in the jaw and promote relaxation of the TMJ (temporomandibular joint), reducing the likelihood of locking.

Peppermint oil: Peppermint oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking, while its cooling sensation can also reduce muscle spasms and tension in the jaw.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, maypop roots help to reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw lock.
Buds: Maypop buds contain flavonoids that have been shown to relax facial muscles and ease tension in the temporomandibular joint.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in late summer or early fall, ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours, reducing moisture content to 10%.
Combine 20 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 100 grams of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil and reach optimal strength.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and transferring the liquid to sterilized containers, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with jaw lock because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been traditionally used to ease tension and pain, making it an effective remedy for alleviating jaw discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area, St John's wort cream can provide rapid relief from jaw tightness and lock, allowing for smoother jaw movements and reduced pain.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw locking (TMJ disorder).
Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress and promote healing in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) area.
Tannins: The tannins present in St. John's Wort creams help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can aid in reducing inflammation and swelling in the jaw joint.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing hypericin, a powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension in the jaw.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm the jaw muscles, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried st john's wort leaves and flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground st john's wort powder with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated and the wax has melted after 5 minutes of heating.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to around 120°f before whipping the mixture with a hand whisk or electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterile jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension. The herb's potent compounds, such as thujone and camphor, work together to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, releasing pressure and tension that can cause locking or clicking sensations. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, providing relief from jaw pain and stiffness. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Wormwood creams contain compounds that relax muscle spasms, helping to reduce tension and alleviate jaw locking (TMJ) symptoms by calming the muscles responsible for tooth clenching and grinding.
Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which can contribute to jaw locking and discomfort.
Analgesic: Wormwood creams contain natural analgesics that numb the area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking, allowing for easier movement and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, wormwood roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax tense jaw muscles.
Barks: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes, wormwood barks have been traditionally used to ease jaw tension and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it thin on paper bags or trays for two weeks.
Combine one part of dried artemisia absinthium with four parts of coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 50°c for four hours to allow for decarboxylation and infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar for storage and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with jaw lock because it contains anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties that ease tension in the jaw muscles. The cream's soothing ingredients, such as camphor and menthol, penetrate deep into the skin to calm the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues. By reducing inflammation and relaxating the muscles, herbal elder creams provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with jaw lock, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable chewing and speaking experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, which can contribute to jaw lock and related discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help alleviate pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with TMJ disorders by reducing inflammation in the affected areas.

Muscle-relaxing properties: Elder cream's muscle-relaxing compounds, including mucilage and tannins, can help soothe tense muscles around the jaw and temple area, which is often affected by jaw lock and related conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them effective in alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the elder tree have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the facial muscles, providing relief from jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and 50g of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Combine 200ml of sweet almond oil and 20g of coconut oil in a separate heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the beeswax in the microwave for 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval until fully liquefied.
Add the dried sambucus nigra flowers to the melted beeswax mixture and steep for 2-3 hours, then strain the liquid using cheesecloth.
Mix the strained liquid with the sweet almond oil and coconut oil combination, then pour into sterilized containers and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with jaw lock because of its unique properties that target the underlying tension and stress that contribute to this condition. The calming and soothing properties of lemongrass work to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing stiffness and pain associated with TMJ disorders. Additionally, the antispasmodic effects of lemongrass help to relieve spasms and cramps that can cause jaw locking, allowing for smoother and more relaxed movement of the jaw joint.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemongrass cream contains compounds that relax muscle spasms, reducing tension and alleviating jaw lock by calming down the muscles involved in jaw movement.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, allowing for smoother and more comfortable jaw movements.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidants help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of chronic jaw problems like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stems of lemongrass are used to make medicinal creams for jaw lock due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension.
Barks: Lemongrass barks have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to alleviate toothache, jaw pain, and tension, making them a suitable choice for medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered cymbopogon citratus with 250ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of beeswax and stir until fully incorporated and melted, then remove from heat after 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 1 hour before whipping with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Fill sterilized jars with the cream and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with jaw lock because they provide soothing relief to the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), a common culprit behind jaw locking. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera gel calm irritated muscles and reduce tension, allowing for smoother joint movement. Additionally, aloe's natural moisturizing abilities help keep the skin around the jawline hydrated and supple, further alleviating discomfort. Regular application of herbal aloe creams can lead to increased flexibility and reduced pain associated with jaw locking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints, easing tension and discomfort associated with jaw locks.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of aloe vera helps neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw area, contributing to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Soften and relaxes muscles property: Aloe vera's mucilages and polysaccharides have a soothing effect on the skin and mucous membranes, helping to soften and relax tight muscles in the jaw, neck, and face that can contribute to jaw locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
Barks: The barks of the aloe plant have analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving toothache and gum pain caused by jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves weighing at least 100 grams.
Combine the harvested aloe vera gel with 20 grams of grated ginger root in a blender.
Add 15 grams of chamomile flowers and 10 grams of calendula petals to the blended mixture.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a sterilized container and allow it to solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with jaw lock because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's calming properties help to reduce muscle tension, relax the jaw, and ease the discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, pot marigold creams provide relief from jaw pain, stiffness, and limited range of motion, making them a natural and effective treatment for jaw lock sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula, the main active compound in pot marigold, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw locks.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the jaw joint, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Wound-healing property: Calendula's ability to promote wound healing can aid in the repair of damaged tissues in the jaw joint, which can alleviate tension and relieve symptoms of jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw area.
Barks: The barks of the pot marigold plant have astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate tension in the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator for 12 hours or sun drying for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried calendula petals into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered calendula with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a glass jar.
Allow the infused oil to cool to room temperature before whipping with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a mixing bowl until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jaw lock because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that target the source of tension in the jaw. The creams can penetrate deep into the muscles and joints, soothing and relaxing them. The dandelion extract also has natural analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, these creams provide long-lasting relief from TMJ disorders and habitual clenching of the jaw.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw muscles, alleviating tension and pain associated with jaw lock.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of dandelion can help relax muscle spasms in the jaw, releasing tension and allowing for a smoother, more comfortable movement of the jaw.
Analgesic: Dandelion cream's analgesic properties can provide relief from pain associated with jaw lock, reducing discomfort and soreness in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help soothe and calm irritated jaw tissue.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive molecules like sesquiterpenes, rhyzomes have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before mixing with 50g of beeswax.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool to solidify and thicken the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique combination of soothing and calming properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as Ginkgo biloba and other herbal extracts, work together to reduce muscle tension and ease the discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, users can experience quick relief from jaw pain, stiffness, and clicking or popping sounds, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed bite.
remedy_properties: Arnica: Arnica contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw muscles and joints.

Valerian: Valerian root has been traditionally used for its sedative and relaxing properties, which may help to calm tense muscles and improve sleep quality, potentially reducing jaw lock symptoms caused by stress or anxiety.
Peppermint: Peppermint oil contains menthol, which can help to relax muscles and reduce pain in the jaw area. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate joint discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks exhibit analgesic and antiseptic properties, making them effective in relieving jaw lock discomfort.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, seeds have anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe and calm the jaw muscles, reducing tension and pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted and combined.
Remove from heat and let cool for 15 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store in refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baby rubber plant creams helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to relax and soothe the muscles in the face and jaw. The calming properties of these herbs help to reduce tension and inflammation, allowing the jaw joint to function properly and alleviating symptoms of TMJ disorders such as locking or clicking. Regular use of these creams can provide long-lasting relief from jaw lock and promote overall oral health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): May help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating symptoms of TMD.
Ginger: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which may help soothe jaw pain and discomfort.
Lavender: Known for its calming and relaxing properties, lavender oil or extract might help reduce stress and tension in the jaw muscles, contributing to a lockjaw-like sensation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of alkaloids that help relax tense muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried lavender buds.
Combine the dried herbs with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler setup on medium heat.
Stir gently for 10 minutes or until the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and mix until melted, then pour into jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with jaw lock because of its cooling and relaxing properties. When applied topically, the menthol in peppermint oil can help ease tension and spasms in the jaw muscles, providing immediate relief from locking or grinding sensations. As the cream soothes and calms the affected area, it also reduces inflammation and promotes blood flow, allowing for a more natural alignment of the teeth and jawbone, thus resolving the lock. Regular use of herbal peppermint creams can lead to a significant reduction in frequency and severity of jaw locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and surrounding muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of peppermint can numb the pain associated with jaw lock, allowing for temporary relief from discomfort and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds, such as borneol, can help relax tense muscles in the jaw, face, and neck, which can contribute to jaw lock and related issues like migraines and TMJ disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing jaw lock pain.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain a high concentration of menthol, which provides immediate relief from jaw tension and muscle spasms associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried peppermint leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground peppermint powder with 8 ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over medium heat.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to ensure the infusion process is complete.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Whip 8 ounces of coconut cream with the infused peppermint oil and add 2 drops of lavender essential oil for an additional 3 minutes until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique combination of natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds. The cream's active ingredients, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work together to reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, alleviating the discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. As a result, users can experience significant relief from jaw lock, allowing for more comfortable eating, speaking, and overall relaxation of the facial and jaw muscles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as polyphenols and terpenes, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chronic jaw tension and lockjaw.

Muscle relaxant properties: Kansas coneflower contains compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes that have been shown to relax muscle tissue and reduce spasms, making it helpful in alleviating the discomfort and stiffness associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw tension and pain.
Barks: The barks contain triterpenoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing jaw discomfort and lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers at the rate of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of cream base.
Combine the harvested flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids; set aside for later use.
Mix the strained oil with 8 ounces of unscented cream base and 2 teaspoons of beeswax, heating until melted and blended in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with jaw lock because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that soothes and calms inflamed tissues. When applied topically to the affected area, it reduces swelling and relaxes tense muscles, thus alleviating tension and discomfort caused by TMJ disorder. Additionally, milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and stiffness in the jaw joint, allowing for smoother movement and relief from lockjaw symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal milk thistle creams contain flavonoids, such as silymarin, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw locks (TMJ disorders).

Antioxidant: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by inflammation, promoting overall health and reducing symptoms of jaw lock.

Silymarin: Silymarin, a bioflavonoid found in milk thistle, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can specifically target and reduce pain and stiffness associated with jaw locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain silymarin, which helps reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension in the jaw, alleviating lockjaw symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried silybum marianum leaves and stems from a trusted source or storehouse.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered silybum marianum with 30 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the muscles in the face and neck. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce tension and swelling, allowing for a more relaxed state of the jaw and surrounding areas. Additionally, wild geranium's natural analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking. By applying a cream containing wild geranium extract, individuals can experience relief from tension headaches, migraines, and TMJ disorders, promoting overall facial relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild geranium contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) area, alleviating jaw lock symptoms.

Antispasmodic: The essential oils present in wild geranium, such as geraniol and linalool, exhibit antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscle tension and reduce spasms in the jaw muscles that contribute to jaw lock.
Analgesic: Wild geranium has been traditionally used to relieve pain, and its analgesic properties may help numb or reduce discomfort associated with jaw lock, making it easier to manage symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild geranium plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw lock.

Barks: The barks of the wild geranium plant are rich in tannins, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried geranium maculatum leaves and stems from local organic farms or your own garden.
Combine the dried herbs with 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan, placing it on low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets with 1/8 cup of the infused oil in a small bowl, heating gently until melted and smooth.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer to create a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with jaw lock because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and relax the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw. The herb angelica itself has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing tension and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching and locking. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, allowing for easier movement and reduced pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which can contribute to jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: The essential oils present in angelica, such as bergapten, have analgesic and anesthetic properties that can help alleviate discomfort and numbness associated with jaw lock.
Antispasmodic: Angelica's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing tension and spasms that can cause jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax tense muscles in the jaw.
Barks: Contain volatile oils with analgesic and antiseptic properties, reducing pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, providing nourishment to the skin and promoting healthy tissue growth for a relaxed jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried angelica archangelica root and 1 ounce of beeswax pellets.
Combine the dried angelica archangelica root with 4 ounces of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the herbs release their properties into the oil.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of jojoba oil to the cooled herbal infusion and whip until emulsified.
Mix the whipped cream base with 8 ounces of coconut milk and 2 ounces of shea butter, then pour into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with jaw lock because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. The calming properties of the herbs, such as chamomile and lavender, help to soothe the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking. Additionally, the creams' anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the jaw joint, providing long-lasting relief from tension headaches, migraines, and other symptoms related to jaw lock.
remedy_properties: Ashwagandha: Known for its anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties, ashwagandha may help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles, alleviating jaw locks.
Lavender: Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system and can help ease muscle spasms, including those in the jaw, promoting relaxation and reducing tension.
Ginger: Ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw locks, as well as promote digestion and reduce stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and terpenes that provide analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties, helping to relieve tension and discomfort in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and leaves for use in this recipe.
Infuse dried rosa rugosa petals in carrier oil for 4 weeks at room temperature.
Combine infused oil with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 160°f while stirring occasionally for 30 minutes then cool.
Whip the cooled mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jaw lock because its calming properties soothe tension in the muscles of the face, particularly the masseter muscle responsible for jaw clenching. The cream's anti-inflammatory and relaxing effects ease stiffness and discomfort, allowing the jaw to release its tight grip. As a result, the cream provides quick relief from stress-induced jaw locking, promoting relaxation and reducing the risk of chronic pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which often cause jaw locking.
Muscle relaxant: The calming effects of lavender can help relax tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing spasms that contribute to jaw locking and discomfort.
Anxiolytic: Lavender's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety can help alleviate stress-induced clenching and grinding habits that lead to jaw locking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of English lavender help soothe and relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and pain associated with jaw lock.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks of English lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that ease jaw discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of cream.
Combine 200g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a dark glass jar and let it infuse for 6 hours.
Strain the infused vodka mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20ml of the infused vodka extract in a double boiler and heat until fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to 60°c before transferring the mixture into glass jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaw lock are topical solutions that utilize natural ingredients to soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw, providing relief from tension headaches, migraines, and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. These creams contain herbs like arnica, peppermint, and ginger, which reduce inflammation and ease spasms, allowing for improved flexibility and reduced discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that help with jaw lock include those containing witch hazel, chamomile, and lavender. By using these creams, individuals can alleviate chronic jaw pain, improve sleep quality, and regain confidence in their ability to eat, speak, and smile without discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw lock with herbal creams is to apply a topical cream containing ingredients like camphor, menthol, or peppermint oil. These natural analgesics help reduce tension and ease muscle spasms, providing temporary relief from jaw locking sensations. Massage the cream onto the affected area for 2-3 minutes before sleep to promote relaxation and alleviate discomfort overnight.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw lock that are treated with herbal creams are various musculoskeletal issues, such as tennis elbow, carpal tunnel syndrome, and fibromyalgia. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, St. John's Wort, and capsaicin help alleviate pain and inflammation in these conditions by reducing muscle tension, improving circulation, and stimulating the release of natural pain-relievers in the body. These topical treatments offer a safe and effective alternative to oral medications and surgery.
